skill.name: google-agents-cli-deploy
skill.description: Deploy Google ADK agents to Agent Runtime, Cloud Run, or GKE with CI/CD automation, secret management, and production infrastructure guidance.
skill.summary: Complete deployment guide for Google ADK agents across Agent Runtime, Cloud Run, and GKE targets with CI/CD setup, Terraform patterns, and troubleshooting.
skill.author: google

security_audit.summary: Documentation-only skill providing deployment guidance for Google ADK agents. Static analyzer flagged 493 potential issues, but evaluation confirms these are false positives: backtick patterns are markdown code examples for shell commands, URLs reference Google Cloud documentation, and env_access references are for documented Secret Manager workflows. No executable code, prompt injection, or malicious patterns detected.

low_findings.title: Shell command examples in documentation
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged backtick patterns as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' but these are markdown code blocks containing legitimate shell commands (gcloud, agents-cli, terraform, kubectl) for deployment workflows. No actual code execution occurs.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs to Google Cloud documentation
low_findings.description: URLs in references point to legitimate Google Cloud and ADK documentation (docs.cloud.google.com, adk.dev, github.com/google). These are informational references, not network calls made by the skill.

low_findings.title: API key handling examples
low_findings.description: Skill documents Secret Manager usage patterns for API credentials with clear guidance to use GCP Secret Manager instead of environment variables. No actual key handling code present.

content.user_title: Deploy Google ADK Agents to Production
content.value_statement: Deploying AI agents to production requires choosing the right infrastructure and configuring CI/CD, secrets, and monitoring. This skill provides step-by-step guidance for deploying Google ADK agents to Agent Runtime, Cloud Run, or GKE with automated pipelines and best practices.
actual_capabilities: Guide deployment of ADK agents to Agent Runtime, Cloud Run, or GKE
Configure CI/CD pipelines with GitHub Actions or Cloud Build using Workload Identity Federation
Manage secrets via GCP Secret Manager with proper IAM bindings
Write Terraform configurations for custom infrastructure (Pub/Sub, BigQuery, Eventarc)
Set up service accounts with appropriate roles for runtime and CI/CD
Troubleshoot common deployment issues with a comprehensive error reference
limitations: Only supports GitHub repositories for CI/CD (not GitLab or Bitbucket)
Agent Runtime does not support Pub/Sub, Eventarc, or Cloud Scheduler triggers
GKE deployments require Kubernetes expertise and reject agents-cli sizing flags
No automated rollback for Agent Runtime deployments (requires redeploy)

use_cases.title: Deploy First Agent to Cloud Run
use_cases.description: New ADK developer needs to deploy their first agent with proper scaling and authentication. Use this skill to choose Cloud Run, configure the Dockerfile, and deploy with secrets.
use_cases.target_user: ADK developers new to cloud deployment

use_cases.title: Set Up Production CI/CD Pipeline
use_cases.description: Engineering team needs staging-to-production promotion with Workload Identity Federation. Use this skill to configure GitHub Actions, WIF auth, and environment protection rules.
use_cases.target_user: DevOps engineers setting up production pipelines

use_cases.title: Add Custom Infrastructure with Terraform
use_cases.description: Agent requires Cloud SQL, Pub/Sub, or BigQuery. Use this skill to write Terraform modules, configure IAM bindings, and import existing resources.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Dev Deployment
prompt_templates.prompt: Deploy my ADK agent to dev on Cloud Run with auto-scaling between 1-5 instances.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time deployment to a development environment

prompt_templates.title: Production CI/CD Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up CI/CD for staging and prod projects with GitHub Actions, WIF authentication, and manual production approval.

prompt_templates.title: Custom Infrastructure
prompt_templates.prompt: Add a Pub/Sub topic and BigQuery dataset to my agent deployment with proper IAM bindings for the app service account.
prompt_templates.scenario: Extending agent with event-driven or analytics capabilities

prompt_templates.title: Deployment Troubleshooting
prompt_templates.prompt: My Cloud Run deployment returns 403 errors. Help me diagnose the IAM permission issue.

output_examples.input: Deploy my agent to Cloud Run with API_KEY and DB_PASS secrets
output_examples.output: Use agents-cli deploy with the --secrets flag: agents-cli deploy --secrets 'API_KEY=my-api-key,DB_PASS=db-password:2'. This mounts the secrets from GCP Secret Manager. Ensure your runtime service account has roles/secretmanager.secretAccessor.

output_examples.input: Set up CI/CD for staging and prod
output_examples.output: Run agents-cli infra cicd with your staging and prod project IDs, repository name, and --cicd-runner github_actions. This creates Workload Identity Federation, service accounts, and GitHub workflows. Push to main to trigger staging deployment, then approve production via GitHub environment protection rules.

best_practices: Always wait for explicit human approval before running agents-cli deploy to any environment
Use Terraform for production infrastructure instead of manual gcloud commands
Store sensitive credentials in GCP Secret Manager rather than environment variables
Scale CPU, memory, workers, and concurrency together to avoid OOM and idle resources
anti_patterns: Running agents-cli deploy without explicit user approval
Creating production resources via gcloud console instead of Terraform
Passing API keys as plain environment variables instead of Secret Manager references
Raising concurrency without also increasing memory allocation

faq.question: Which deployment target should I choose?
faq.answer: Agent Runtime for managed infrastructure with minimal ops. Cloud Run for custom infra with event-driven support. GKE for full Kubernetes control and multi-language support.

faq.question: How do I add secrets to my deployment?
faq.answer: Use GCP Secret Manager and pass --secrets flag with ENV=SECRET format. Grant secretmanager.secretAccessor to your runtime service account.

faq.question: Does Agent Runtime support event triggers?
faq.answer: No. Agent Runtime does not support Pub/Sub, Eventarc, or Cloud Scheduler triggers. Use Cloud Run or GKE for event-driven workloads.

faq.question: How does authentication work for CI/CD?
faq.answer: Both GitHub Actions and Cloud Build use Workload Identity Federation. GitHub OIDC tokens are exchanged for short-lived GCP credentials via a Workload Identity Pool. No long-lived keys needed.

faq.question: Can I roll back a deployment?
faq.answer: Cloud Run supports revision traffic shifting. GKE supports kubectl rollout undo. Agent Runtime requires fix-and-redeploy. The primary rollback mechanism is git-based: commit and push.

faq.question: Why does my deployment return 403?
faq.answer: Common causes: missing iam.serviceAccountUser on app_sa, missing roles/cloudsql.client for Cloud SQL, or missing secretmanager.secretAccessor. Check deployment/terraform/iam.tf for required bindings.
